You can use the Array.from() method to convert a NodeList to an array:

const elements = document.querySelectorAll('.item');
const arr = Array.from(elements);
arr.forEach(el => console.log(el.textContent));

Alternatively, use the spread operator:

const arr = [...document.querySelectorAll('.item')];

Both approaches work in modern browsers. For older browser support, use Array.prototype.slice.call(nodeList).

Why not for...of directly?

In older TypeScript configurations without lib: dom, NodeListOf<T> is not considered iterable. Always use Array.from() to be safe.
